title: celmisia alpina
intro: Celmisia alpina, also known as the Alpine Daisy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Celmisia alpina can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and has been used in traditional herbal practices in various parts of the world.
